transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good morning, First National Bank. I'm Femi. Can I start by confirming your name and date of birth?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Morning. Petra Kovacs, fourth of April 1989.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Petra, you're verified. What's happened today?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: I've done something really stupid — I sent money to the wrong account. Four hundred and fifty pounds. It was my sister's old account number that she's since closed.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Don't worry, we can deal with this. How long ago was the transfer?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Do you know which bank your sister uses?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Even better — an intra-bank recall is usually quicker. I'm raising it now. Reference CAN-2024-8010. Because the account was closed, Nationwide should automatically return the funds within forty-eight hours since closed accounts can't hold balances.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Oh, that's actually reassuring. So it should come back on its own?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: The recall ensures it's formally tracked and handled. I've also sent you an email confirmation. Is there anything else?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Thank you so much Femi. That's a massive relief.
